entry.title: ETSI Mission Critical 10th Plugtests Report Unveiled - ETSI
entry.summary: More than 250 participants from around the world gathered to discuss advancing the transition to quantum-safe security.

entry.title: Invitation to comment on Akoma Ntoso v2.0 Part 2 (AKN 3.1) before call for consent as OASIS Standard
entry.summary: OASIS and the OASIS LegalDocumentML (LegalDocML) TC [1] are pleased to announce that Akoma Ntoso Version 2.0. Part 2: Specifications of AKN 3.1 CS01 is now available for public review and comment before a call for consent as OASIS Standard. Akoma Ntoso defines an XML vocabulary for parliamentary, legislative, and judicial documents, enabling machine-readable exchange […]

entry.title: Become a voice for global technology standards: launching the ETSI Ambassador programme - ETSI
entry.summary: More than 250 participants from around the world gathered to discuss advancing the transition to quantum-safe security.

entry.title: Invitation to comment on KMIP Usage Guide v3.0 – ends 15 August 2026
entry.summary: OASIS and the KMIP TC are pleased to announce that the Key Management Interoperability Protocol Usage Guide Version 3.0 is now available for public review and comment.

entry.title: OASIS Approves Two Public-Key Cryptography Standards to Advance Post-Quantum Security and Interoperability
entry.summary: Boston, MA – 15 July 2026 – OASIS Open, the international open source and standards consortium, announced that PKCS #11 Specification Version 3.2 and PKCS #11 Profiles Version 3.2 have been approved as OASIS Standards. This milestone reflects the highest level of ratification and strengthens the widely deployed Cryptoki (cryptographic token interface) API, which underpins […]

entry.title: Invitation to comment on KMIP Specification v3.0 and KMIP Profiles v3.0 - ends 13 August 2026
entry.summary: OASIS and the KMIP TC are pleased to announce that Key Management Interoperability Protocol Specification Version 3.0 and Key Management Interoperability Protocol Profiles Version 3.0 are now available for public review and comment.

entry.title: Invitation to comment on OpenEoX Core Schema Version 1.0 CSD01
entry.summary: OASIS and the OpenEoX TC are pleased to announce that OpenEoX Core Schema Version 1.0 CSD01 is now available for public review and comment. The OpenEoX Core Schema defines the core schema for the OpenEoX unified machine-readable approach to managing and sharing General Availability (GA), End-of-Sales (EoS), End-of-Life (EoL), and End-of-Security-Support (EoSSec) information.

entry.title: Wait & see is not an option: Q-day is here - ETSI
entry.summary: More than 250 participants from around the world gathered to discuss advancing the transition to quantum-safe security.

entry.title: ISO launches public consultation on the world’s first International Standard for net zero alignment today
entry.summary: London, England | 17 June 2026 – Ahead of London Climate Action Week, the International Organization for Standardization (ISO) has today launched the public consultation for the ISO Net Zero Aligned Organizations Standard (ISO 14060), the world’s first international independently verifiable standard …

entry.title: ETSI unveils winners of the Research & Innovation Awards 2026 - ETSI
entry.summary: Announced last night in Málaga during the gala dinner of the EuCNC & 6G Summit, the winners were recognised in three categories.

entry.title: NIEM Goes Global: NIEMOpen Submits to ISO/IEC JTC 1
entry.summary: By Paul Wormeli, Chair, NIEMOpen Project Governing Board OASIS Open has formally submitted two NIEMOpen standards to ISO/IEC Joint Technical Committee 1 (JTC 1) for transposition into International Standards. The two submissions are NIEM Model v6.0 and the Naming and Design Rules (NDR) v6.0, both approved as OASIS Standards in December 2025. This is a significant moment. […]

entry.title: ETSI launches TeraFlowSDN Release 7 - ETSI
entry.summary: ETSI is pleased to announce the launch of TeraFlowSDN Release 7. With a broad range of new features spanning P4-device integration, sophisticated automation for multi-granular optical networks, and expanded Northbound Interface (NBI) capabilities, this release positions TeraFlowSDN as the most advanced open-source Network Automation Framework.

entry.title: ETSI UCAAT 2026 returns to ETSI Headquarters, spotlighting AI quality and the future of testing - ETSI
entry.summary: After 11 years, the ETSI User Conference on Advanced Automated Testing (UCAAT), held from 14 to 16 April 2026, returned to ETSI headquarters, making one of the most successful and interactive editions of the event.

entry.title: Homage to Michel Mouly, one of the founders of GSM - ETSI
entry.summary: ETSI is deeply saddened to learn of the passing of Michel Mouly. His funeral was held in Paris on 30 April 2026. A pillar of the GSM community and the standards work, Michel was involved in GSM from its very […]

entry.title: IEC and ETSI Renew Their Cooperation Agreement - ETSI
entry.summary: Today, at the IEC headquarters in Geneva, ETSI Director-General Jan Ellsberger and IEC Secretary-General & CEO Philippe Metzger have renewed a Cooperation Agreement to reinforce collaboration and coordination in the development of international and regional standards in the area of information and communication technologies.

entry.title: From reporting to results: Rethinking environmental management with ISO 14001
entry.summary: In a world where environmental expectations are rising as fast as the challenges themselves, organizations face a defining shift: it is no longer enough to state commitments, they must demonstrate how those commitments translate into action and results. ISO 14001:2026 comes at this critical moment.

entry.title: ETSI’s Response to the European Commission’s Proposal for the Cybersecurity Act 2 - ETSI
entry.summary: ETSI published a response to the European Commission’s Proposal for the Cybersecurity Act 2. Read our full response here.

entry.title: ISO 14001:2026 published – raising the bar for environmental performance
entry.summary: The new edition of ISO 14001 has been released, giving organizations a clearer, more effective way to turn environmental ambition into measurable results.

entry.title: ETSI General Assembly: the time for ETSI is now! - ETSI
entry.summary: ETSI concluded its latest General Assembly with a forward‑looking agenda that highlighted the organisation’s strategic transformation, reaffirmed its alignment with EU priorities, and celebrated outstanding contributions within the ETSI community through the 2026 ETSI Fellows Awards.

entry.title: Why resilient infrastructure can no longer be optional
entry.summary: In my three decades of responding to catastrophic events around the world, I have often observed a curious human tendency to overlook the invisible foundations of our safety. We are meticulous about the visible – the aesthetic finish, convenience and efficiency of our cities – while neglecting the invisible …

entry.title: New ISO standards bring clarity to chain of custody
entry.summary: Building confidence in sustainability claims worldwide.

entry.title: Building a net-zero metals future with harmonized standards
entry.summary: The path to a net-zero future depends on every industry doing its part, and few are more pivotal than the steel and aluminium sectors. But they have a cost, particularly when it comes to their carbon footprint.

entry.title: ISO - Media kit
entry.summary: ISO (International Organization for Standardization) is the world’s largest developer and publisher of International Standards. ISO is a network of national standards bodies of 175 countries, with a Central Secretariat in Geneva, Switzerland.

entry.title: IETF Community Survey 2025
entry.summary: Each year the IETF Community Survey provides a comprehensive assessment of community demographics, engagement patterns, and perceptions of organizational effectiveness. The full report from the 2025 edition is now available.

entry.title: Birds of a Feather at IETF 126
entry.summary: The IETF 126 Vienna meeting takes place 18–24 July 2026. As at every IETF meeting, alongside the established Working Groups there will be a handful of Birds-of-a-Feather (BoF) sessions—and these are often the most interesting place to watch where Internet standards work is heading next.

entry.title: From Lab to RFC: A PhD Student's Journey through the IETF
entry.summary: Martine Sophie Lenders has been regularly participating in the IETF for over a decade, starting with the IETF 93 meeting in Prague in 2015. She has authored several Internet-Drafts—two of which recently were published as RFCs—and at the same time works on a PhD at FU Berlin and as a research associate at TU Dresden. We asked a few questions about what her experience in the IETF has been like while pursuing an academic journey.

entry.title: Suggested IETF 126 Sessions for Getting Familiar with New Topics
entry.summary: These IETF 126 meeting sessions are likely to include discussions and proposals that are accessible to a broad range of Internet technologists whether they are new to the IETF or long-time participants.

entry.title: IETF LLC Board Retreat 2026
entry.summary: The IETF Administration LLC Board of Directors held its annual retreat 29-30 April 2026 in Amsterdam. In addition to all Board members, the IETF Executive Director, the Director of Finance, and the Board Secretary were present. Here is a short summary of the main points we discussed.

entry.title: IETF Administration LLC 2025 Annual Financial Audit
entry.summary: IETF Administration LLC Board of Directors received from external auditors the report of a clean result for its 2025 annual financial statement.

entry.title: New RFC Editor website is live
entry.summary: Today we are launching the new rfc-editor.org website, the most visible part of a comprehensive overhaul of the tools that support editing and publishing RFCs.

entry.title: IETF 125 Highlights
entry.summary: More than 1500 participants gathered in Shenzhen and online for the IETF 125 meeting 14-20 March 2026 for more than 100 working sessions, an IETF Hackathon, and more.

entry.title: Report from the 2026 RPC Retreat
entry.summary: The RFC Production Center (RPC) retreat was a two-day strategic planning session taking place the week of April 20 that gathered the entire RPC team and IETF Administration senior staff.

entry.title: A new suite of modern tools coming for editing and publishing RFCs
entry.summary: After a number of years of work, the initial rollout of a modern suite of tools for editing and publishing RFCs, including an entirely new rfc-editor.org website, will take place in May 2026.

entry.title: W3C Invites Implementations of EPUB 3.4, EPUB Reading Systems 3.4 and EPUB Accessibility 1.2
entry.summary: EPUB® 3 defines a distribution and interchange format for digital publications and documents. The EPUB format provides a means of representing, packaging, and encoding structured and semantically enhanced web content — including HTML, CSS, SVG, and other resources — for distribution in a single-file container.

entry.title: Proposed Advancement of Web Authentication: An API for accessing Public Key Credentials Level 3 to W3C Recommendation
entry.summary: This specification defines an API enabling the creation and use of strong, attested, scoped, public key-based credentials by web applications, for the purpose of strongly authenticating users.

entry.title: First Public Working Draft: Character Model for the World Wide Web: String Matching
entry.summary: This document builds upon Character Model for the World Wide Web 1.0: Fundamentals to provide authors of specifications, software developers, and content developers a common reference on string identity matching on the World Wide Web and thereby increase interoperability.

entry.title: First Public Working Draft: Strings on the Web: Language and Direction Metadata
entry.summary: This document describes the best practices for identifying the language and direction for strings used on the Web.

entry.title: First Public Working Draft: CSS Linked Parameters Module Level 1
entry.summary: This spec introduces a way to pass CSS values into linked resources, such as SVG images, so that they can be used as CSS custom environment variables in the destination resource.

entry.title: Group Note Draft: Linked Web Storage Vocabulary
entry.summary: This document describes the Linked Web Storage Vocabulary, i.e., the RDF Schema vocabulary used by the Linked Web Storage Protocol.

entry.title: Group Note Draft: What’s New in RDF 1.2
entry.summary: This document provides the reader with a summary of changes to RDF introduced in RDF version 1.2.

entry.title: First Public Working Draft: SHACL 1.2 Profiling
entry.summary: SHACL Profiling defines elements of the SHACL Shapes Constraint Language created to allow for profiles of SHACL and profiling with SHACL.

entry.title: Pointer Events Level 3 is now a W3C Recommendation
entry.summary: The features in this specification extend or modify those found in Pointer Events, a W3C Recommendation that describes events and related interfaces for handling hardware-agnostic pointer input from devices including a mouse, pen, or touchscreen.

entry.title: Group Note Draft: W3C Standards Vulnerability Disclosure & Handling Process and Policy
entry.summary: This document defines how to report suspected security vulnerabilities in W3C standards and specifications (technical reports), so that issues can be triaged, confirmed, and resolved through the appropriate W3C processes.

entry.title: News
entry.summary: IEEE Standards Association News, Press Releases and Blog

entry.title: IEEE SA Newsletter
entry.summary: The IEEE SA Newsletter is a newsletter on standards and related solutions. Learn about new developments, including resources, insights and more.

entry.title: 2025 News Archive
entry.summary: View a compilation of Press Releases, News Articles, and other media related to IEEE Standards Association in 2025.

entry.title: News from OASIS Open and our communities
entry.summary: Stay current on the major announcements, project milestones, and new activities happening at OASIS.

entry.title: News from OASIS Open and our communities
entry.summary: Stay current on the major announcements, project milestones, and new activities happening at OASIS.

entry.title: News from OASIS Open and our communities
entry.summary: Stay current on the major announcements, project milestones, and new activities happening at OASIS.

entry.title: News from OASIS Open and our communities
entry.summary: Stay current on the major announcements, project milestones, and new activities happening at OASIS.

entry.title: ITU News: Latest from the UN agency for digital technologies
entry.summary: ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​The International Telecommunication Union (ITU) is the United Nations specialized agency for information and communication technologies – ICTs. On ITU Hub you'll find ITU News, publications, and membership information.

entry.title: Subscribe to the ITU Newsletter - ITU
entry.summary: Subscribe to the ITU Newsletter for regular ITU News briefings delivered to your inbox, including ICT trends, digital transformation insights, and upcoming ITU events.

entry.title: News
entry.summary: Check out the latest news and information related to your sectors or topics of interest.
